mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5% threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override growth optimism, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term marginal predictive contribution is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which immediately penalizes equity valuations and generates negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation observations within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly correlates with positive corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive industrial production changes coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic momentum that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where payrolls exceed the 200k threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures erode corporate profit margins and trigger risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment environment, rising industrial production indicates tight capacity constraints that force price increases and margin expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when unemployment rises above 4.5%.
